facts.claim: 8 rules across two implementation pathways: v1 facade (RULE-PROFILAXIA-007, full 11-criterion catalog) and v3 facade (RULE-PROFILAXIA-008, reduced 2-criterion subset); v1 predicates for VTE/insulin (RULE-001/002) were extracted as formula/decision-tree; v3 predicates for stress-ulcer/invasive-device (RULE-005/006).

By type: formula=1 (insulin dosing), decision-tree=7 (dosing logic, alert aggregation, eligibility predicates, facades). By status: OK=6, DISCREPANCY=1 (RULE-005), AMBIGUOUS=1 (RULE-008). By verdict: VERIFIED=2 (RULE-001/002), DISCREPANCY=1, NOT_APPLICABLE=5 (aggregators, facades).

Themes: pharmacotherapy (insulins NPH/regular, LMWH/UFH/enoxaparina, PPIs), device-associated infection prevention (central lines, Foley catheters, hemodialysis lines), GI stress-ulcer prophylaxis (LAMGD), early mobilization, clinical-alert escalation (AMARELO/VERMELHO/NEUTRO severity tiers).

RULE-PROFILAXIA-005 (stress-ulcer eligibility predicate) combines multiple verification gaps: 3 time-window mismatches (48h→50h, 24h→26h, +50h filter not doc'd), 1 string-concat bug ("hemorragicoGrande queimado" fuse), 1 dead-code diagnosis-list check (fromkeys iterates keys not field values). Clinical impact MODERATE; false-negative risk via inert diagnosis branch.

RULE-PROFILAXIA-008 (v3 facade, status AMBIGUOUS) embeds dead code: commented-out SEPSE thresholds (VMI <24h, DVA <6h, PAS<90, TEC>3s, lactato>=3, oliguria) have zero relation to prophylaxis intent; unclear whether incomplete v3 migration or placeholder never removed. Medium-confidence intent drift.

v1↔v3 pathway divergence: v1 facade (RULE-007) explicitly activates 11 criteria (1-3,6,9-11) with recommendations; v3 facade (RULE-008) activates only 2 (criterio_1 LAMGD, criterio_9 device-bundle). Criteria 2-8 exist as commented-out code in v3 model. Unclear whether intentional scope narrowing or incomplete port; blocks SUPERSEDE determination.

constraints.text: RULE-PROFILAXIA-005 (DISCREPANCY verdict) MUST be routed to RATIFY before adoption — diagnosis-list inertness requires clinical intent confirmation (stress-ulcer indication by admission diagnosis vs implementation gap).

MUST preserve dosing coefficients verbatim with published anchors: insulin NPH 0.3–0.5 U/kg/day (RABBIT-2 protocol), enoxaparina 40mg (standard VTE) and 60mg (BMI>40), UFH 5000U q8h/q12h for renal impairment (ACCP/ASH).

MUST preserve PT-BR alert severity colors (AMARELO/VERMELHO/NEUTRO) and clinical terminology verbatim: LAMGD (stress-ulcer prophylaxis), IBP (PPI inibidor), IRAs (infecções relacionadas assistência), PAVM (pneumonia ventilação mecânica).
MUST NOT adopt v3 facade (RULE-008) as-is until dead code (SEPSE thresholds) is removed and v1↔v3 intent disparity is resolved. Recommend ADAPT with scope clarification.

open_questions: Is RULE-PROFILAXIA-005's diagnosis-list check intentionally disabled (institutional decision to omit diagnosis-of-admission criterion), or undetected bug from legacy port?
Should v1 facade (RULE-007, 11 active criteria) be the canonical recommendation source, with v3 facade (RULE-008, 2 criteria) superseded/retired, or is the narrowing intentional for v3 workflow?
Do the 48h→50h and 24h→26h window adjustments in RULE-005 reflect deliberate buffer thresholds or documentation drift?
